name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-31
title: Rename /rabbit-config human-approval subcommand to bypass-human-approval for parallel semantics with bypass-permissions
description:
Naming asymmetry between the two safety-gate subcommands creates confusion:

Current state:
  /rabbit-config human-approval true        → gate ACTIVE (safe/default)
  /rabbit-config human-approval false       → gate BYPASSED (dangerous)
  /rabbit-config bypass-permissions true    → bypass ACTIVE (dangerous)
  /rabbit-config bypass-permissions false   → bypass off (safe/default)

Same boolean value (true) means OPPOSITE outcomes between the two
subcommands. Operators have to mentally invert depending on which
subcommand they're using.

Proposed rename (parallel bypass-X naming):
  /rabbit-config bypass-human-approval true    → bypass ACTIVE
  /rabbit-config bypass-human-approval false   → bypass off (default)
  /rabbit-config bypass-permissions true       → bypass ACTIVE
  /rabbit-config bypass-permissions false      → bypass off (default)

Both subcommands now:
  - Lead with 'bypass-' prefix
  - true = bypass on (dangerous state)
  - false = bypass off (safe/default state)
  - Match the banner text ('HUMAN APPROVAL BYPASS ACTIVE',
    'BYPASS-PERMISSIONS MODE ACTIVE')
  - Match the marker file naming (.rabbit-human-approval-bypass
    already has 'bypass' in it)

WHY KEEP bypass-permissions UNCHANGED:
- Claude Code API literally uses string 'bypassPermissions' as the
  defaultMode value. Our subcommand name should mirror the API to
  prevent mental disconnect between the CLI knob and the underlying
  setting. Renaming bypass-permissions in another direction breaks
  this mapping.

CONTRACT INVARIANT COMPLIANCE:
- Contract Inv 16 forbids 'no-/disable-/skip-/without-' prefixes
  (negation via name). 'bypass-' is NOT a negation prefix — it
  describes the active state of the bypass mode. Both names are
  Inv 16-compliant.
- Contract Inv 15: boolean CLI values use 'true'/'false' exclusively.
  Both subcommands already comply.

SCOPE OF CHANGE:
1. .claude/features/rabbit-cage/skills/rabbit-config/scripts/rabbit-config.py
   - Rename cmd_human_approval → cmd_bypass_human_approval
   - Update argparse subparser name + help text
   - For backwards-compat: optionally accept legacy 'human-approval'
     with a deprecation warning for one cycle, then retire
2. .claude/features/rabbit-cage/skills/rabbit-config/SKILL.md
   - Update CLI surface section (description + body) to use new name
3. .claude/features/rabbit-cage/docs/spec/spec.md
   - Update Inv 55, 56, 57, 68 (reference the subcommand name)
   - Update any other invariants citing 'human-approval' subcommand
   - Update the subcommand description block in the rabbit-config
     section
4. .claude/features/rabbit-cage/docs/spec/contract.md
   - Update any subcommand references
5. Tests
   - Update tests asserting subcommand name
6. Cross-feature references
   - Grep for '/rabbit-config human-approval' across .claude/
   - Update each (likely in rabbit-feature SKILL.md per Step 4
     bypass warning, dispatch-tdd-subagent.py preamble note)
7. Rebuild deployed copies via build.py

MARKER FILE UNCHANGED:
- .rabbit-human-approval-bypass keeps its name (it describes what
  the marker IS, not the subcommand that toggles it)

BANNER TEXT UNCHANGED:
- 'HUMAN APPROVAL BYPASS ACTIVE' already uses the bypass-flavored
  wording

DEPENDENCY:
- BLOCKED on RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-30 (rabbit-cage spec.md monotonic
  renumber) landing first. Both cycles edit rabbit-cage/docs/spec/
  spec.md and would collide if run concurrently. Run AFTER renumber
  cycle merges so this cycle works on the renumbered baseline.

Acceptance:
- /rabbit-config bypass-human-approval CLI works
- /rabbit-config human-approval either (a) errors with helpful
  message pointing at new name, OR (b) accepts with deprecation
  warning, depending on coexistence-window decision at impl time
- Spec invariants reference the new name
- Cross-feature references updated
- All tests pass
- Banner text + marker file unchanged

history.note: TDD cycle complete (secondary item resolved by same commit)
